topic: The metabolic switch — when your body changes fuel
caption:
Ever wondered how your body fuels itself when there's no sugar around? 🤔 It's called the metabolic switch, and it's a survival mechanism that's incredibly powerful for your health! After about 8-12 hours without food, your liver's glycogen stores start to deplete. This triggers a fascinating hormonal shift: insulin drops, and glucagon rises. Suddenly, your body starts converting stored fat into ketone bodies, which become a super-efficient fuel for your brain and muscles. 🧠💪 Understanding this process can help you optimize fat burning, boost energy stability, and improve overall cellular health. It's like having a hidden, highly efficient engine waiting to be used! 🔥

💾 Save this so you don't lose it. Share with someone who needs to understand their body's fuel system better. Follow @healthrecode for daily science-backed health insights. Don't forget — your metabolism is a superpower waiting to be activated!
